title: goodenia occidentalis
intro: Goodenia occidentalis, also known as Western Goodenia, is a type of flowering plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its small blue or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Western Australian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Goodenia occidentalis include its ability to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, wound healing, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of Goodenia occidentalis are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it is also used in traditional remedies such as teas and poultices.
